question.text: What is the carry forward period for losses from exchange-traded derivative transactions?

answer.text: 8 years — losses can be carried forward and set off against any non-speculative business income of subsequent years
Unlimited carry forward period with no expiry
Losses cannot be carried forward — they must be absorbed in the same year

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Since F&O losses are non-speculative business losses (post-2005 amendment), they follow the general business loss carry forward rule of 8 assessment years under Section 72. Speculative losses (if any) can only be carried forward for 4 years under Section 73. **The Trap:** 4 years is for speculative losses. Since derivatives are NON-speculative, the 8-year rule applies. There is no unlimited carry forward for any business loss. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch9, IV Ch9, and VIII Ch9 all specify the 8-year carry forward period. This is universal across exchange-traded derivative segments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** To claim carry forward, the loss must be declared in the tax return filed before the due date. Late returns forfeit the carry forward benefit.
